rulings.Case Title: KENNETH THORNTON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 10, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT KOPPL PIPELINE SERVICES, INC. AMENDED MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Continued to July 30, 2009, per moving party. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CARL BELCASTRO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 10, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT GENSCO INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is granted. Gensco shifts burden and plaintiff did not raise triable issue regarding exposure. Ay Declaration lacks foundation. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY LOPEZ et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 10, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Both motions for summary judgment filed by Ford are denied. The alternative motion for summary adjudication filed by the Nixon Peabody Firm is also denied. Failure to shift burden of production as to exposure and punitive damages. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GERALD BROOKS,SR. VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 10, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT KYLE RAILWAYS, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Is denied. Plaintiff implicitly asserts that it is only seeking to hold Kyle liable for conduct of California Western Railroad. Kyle did not shift the burden regarding plaintiff's ignorance that Kyle is the successor of California Western Railroad at the time he filed the complaint. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD RICE NEMEROVSKI CANADY FALK & RABKIN, A VS. JON KESSLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Notice Of Stay Of Proceeding Submitted Yb The Bar Association Of San Francisco
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 10, 2009, line 14, PLAINTIFF HOWARD RICE NEMEROVSKI CANADY FALK & RABKIN, Motion To Vacate Notice Of Stay Of Proceeding Submitted YB The Bar Association Of San Francisco. Is Granted. Assuming without deciding that email service is insufficient, where, as here, defendants acknowledged receipt of email. Defect in service is waived. e.g. Valov vs. Tank, 168 Cal.App. 3d 867, 876. =(302/HEK)
